message:
fix(config): restore event-emission flag after Lua handler errors

The IS_EVENT registry flag tracks whether code is executing inside a
wezterm.emit() call. When a handler function threw an error, the ?
operator on func.call_async().await? caused an early return from
emit_event, bypassing the flag restoration on line 804. This left
IS_EVENT permanently set to true, corrupting the Lua state for
subsequent emit calls.

Fix: replace early-return ? operators inside the handler loop with
explicit error capture (break on error), ensuring the flag restoration
code always runs before returning.

Validated: 452 config tests pass (was 451 pass + 1 fail).
The cascading failure in term_config_maps_terminal_state_limits is
also resolved (Lua state no longer corrupted between tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
